CREATE TABLE test_numbers (id INT PRIMARY KEY, value FLOAT);
SELECT concat('the LOG10 of 0.1 is: ', cast(log10(0.1) as varchar)) as result;
SELECT concat('the LOG10 of 1 is: ', cast(log10(1) as varchar)) as result;
SELECT concat('the LOG10 of 10 is: ', cast(log10(10) as varchar)) as result;
SELECT concat('the LOG10 of 100 is: ', cast(log10(100) as varchar)) as result;
SELECT concat('the LOG10 of 0.513513 is: ', cast(log10(0.513513) as varchar)) as result;
SELECT id, value, log10(value) as logarithm FROM test_numbers;
SELECT id, value, log10(value) as logarithm FROM test_numbers WHERE value > 0;
CREATE TABLE additional_numbers (identifier INT PRIMARY KEY, number FLOAT);
SELECT a.identifier, a.number, log10(a.number) as log_value, b.logarithm FROM additional_numbers a LEFT JOIN (SELECT id, log10(value) as logarithm FROM test_numbers) b ON a.identifier = b.id;
SELECT concat('the LOG10 of (value + 1) for value = 0.5 is: ', cast(log10(0.5 + 1) as varchar)) as result;
SELECT concat('the LOG10 of (value - 1) for value = 10 is: ', cast(log10(10 - 1) as varchar)) as result;
SELECT concat('the LOG10 of (SELECT MAX(value) FROM test_numbers) is: ', cast(log10((SELECT MAX(value) FROM test_numbers)) as varchar)) as result;
SELECT id, log10(value) AS log10_value FROM (SELECT id, value FROM test_numbers WHERE value < 10) AS sub_query;
SELECT concat('the LOG10 of 1 + 0.1 is: ', cast(log10(1 + 0.1) as varchar)) as result;
